other_findings.finding: Plasma cells are terminally differentiated B cells that secrete antibodies; MZB1 is a key effector of Blimp1 in plasma cell differentiation and integrin function
In skin, plasma cells have been detected in dermis of patients with inflammatory/autoimmune conditions and can secrete IgG locally
JCHAIN+ MZB1+ antibody-secreting cells express JCHAIN, MZB1 and IGKC, which are involved in antibody secretion

evidence_quotes.quote: Antibody-secreting plasma cells are effectors of the humoral immune response. Transcription factor Blimp1 (Prdm1) is essential for the generation and function of plasma cells, and it regulates many genes, including Mzb1
evidence_quotes.source_file: Andreani et al. (2018) Cochaperone Mzb1 is a key effector of Blimp1 in plasma cell differentiation

evidence_quotes.quote: CD138+ plasma cells which can secrete antibodies are also present in the skin of PTM patients. Remarkably, IgG signal can be detected on the surface of these CD138+ plasma cells, suggesting that these plasma cells are secreting antibodies at local lesions
evidence_quotes.source_file: Chen et al. (2021) Local immune microenvironment of skin may play an important role in the development of pretibial myxedema
